name: journey-map
description: Create customer journey maps for user flows and workflows. Use when the user asks to "map the journey", "analyze user flow", "document the workflow", "trace the path", or needs to understand end-to-end user experiences.
capabilities: Guide users through journey mapping methodology
Define user personas and their goals
Structure journey phases from awareness to retention
Document touchpoints with emotions and pain points
Generate emotion curves for user sentiment
author: Dami-Randle
securitySummary: This skill is a pure documentation file containing journey mapping methodology and guidance. The static analysis findings are false positives caused by a broken pattern matcher. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are common English words (emotion, action, retention) incorrectly flagged. The 'backtick execution' findings are ASCII art diagrams, not shell commands. The skill contains no executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or external command execution.
valueStatement: Mapping customer journeys helps teams visualize user experiences and identify where users encounter problems. This skill guides you through documenting user flows to find pain points and opportunities for improvement.
limitations: Does not generate visual diagrams or graphics
Requires user to provide context about their product or service
Does not integrate with external design tools automatically
Does not collect or analyze real user data

useCases.title: Plan feature improvements
useCases.description: Understand user flows to prioritize which features to build or improve based on journey friction points.

useCases.title: Document user experiences
useCases.description: Create standardized journey maps to communicate user experiences and pain points to stakeholders.

useCases.title: Analyze workflow gaps
useCases.description: Map existing user journeys to identify where processes break down or opportunities are missed.

promptTemplates.title: Simple journey map
promptTemplates.prompt: Map the customer journey for [describe user persona] trying to [task or goal]. Include phases from awareness to retention.

promptTemplates.title: Pain point focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a journey map that highlights pain points for [user type] completing [specific task]. Focus on where users feel frustrated or abandon the process.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-touchpoint analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Map every touchpoint for [journey name]. For each step, document the action, interface, user thought, emotion, pain point, and opportunity.

promptTemplates.title: Emotion curve
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an emotion curve journey map showing how user confidence and satisfaction change through [process or flow]. Show highs, lows, and critical moments.

bestPractices: Start with a clear persona definition including their goals, trigger, and success criteria
Document emotions at each step to identify where users feel confident versus frustrated
Focus on actionable opportunities rather than just listing problems
antiPatterns: Creating journeys without defining who the user is and what they want to achieve
Skipping the emotional journey and only documenting actions
Including every single step instead of focusing on meaningful phases and touchpoints

faq.question: What tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill integrates with ux-expert agent for deeper analysis and ux-audit skill for touchpoint evaluation.

faq.question: How long should a journey map be?
faq.answer: Keep it focused. A useful journey map covers 5-12 key steps with context, phases, emotions, and recommendations.

faq.question: Can I use this for B2B workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. Journey mapping works for any user flow including B2B processes, internal tools, and complex multi-step workflows.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill processes only what you provide in the prompt. No data is stored or sent to external services.

faq.question: Why is my journey map too long?
faq.answer: Group detailed steps into phases. Focus on meaningful touchpoints rather than every micro-interaction or click.

faq.question: How is this different from user stories?
faq.answer: Journey maps show the end-to-end experience across time. User stories focus on individual features or tasks in isolation.
